body:
Dear Friend,

After 6 days, 28 stops, and thousands of courageous conservatives
who showed up to greet Ted Cruz, the #CruzinToCaucus bus tour was
a huge success! Throughout the duration of the tour, Ted met with
Iowans in many parts of the Hawkeye State often neglected by
presidential candidates. It was a chance for many Iowa families
to hear from a principled leader who shares and fights for their
values, and it was an opportunity for them to join the building
movement in their home state.

We're seeing
conservatives coalesce around Ted in Iowa and across the nation,
and we continue to work hard to earn every vote. With just a few
short weeks until the caucuses and primaries begin, hundreds of
thousands of Americans have contributed financially, signed up to
volunteer, or joined a leadership team or coalition - please help
us grow that number! Encourage your friends, family, and
neighbors to visit www.tedcruz.org and join us!

Best,

Rick Tyler
Communications
Director

name: Rand Paul
subject: Why Audit the Fed Matters
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for President
body:
Dear Patriot, With Tuesday's vote on Audit the Fed rapidly approaching, I finally received the question I've been waiting for from our national media yesterday morning on CBS' Face the Nation. **" Why does [Audit the Fed] matter to a regular person who may not follow this issue?"** Well, how about income inequality? Or the massive boom-then-bust economic cycles that plague our nation? Or secret bailouts of foreign banks? Or cronyism? Or the fact the Fed's print-now, ask-questions-later policies enable Congress to spend like there's no tomorrow even when taxing and borrowing don't pay the bills? Just take a moment to watch the video below as I explain. [Then with Tuesday's vote rapidly approaching, please agree to your most generous contribution of $50, $25 or even $10 right away.](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32804174:WHXGG9yGN:m:1:879035888:E65B9A12DF14456000F3288859BBAAA3:r) [](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32804175:WHXGG9yGN:m:1:879035888:E65B9A12DF14456000F3288859BBAAA3:r) [With time running out, I simply must be able to count on the support of good folks like you to finally put Audit the Fed on President Obama's desk.](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32804174:WHXGG9yGN:m:1:879035888:E65B9A12DF14456000F3288859BBAAA3:r) **The Fed's PR machine has been working the news media for months now. Their slick lobbyists have been twisting arms on Capital Hill.** While this bill has passed the House with overwhelming bipartisan majorities in the last Congress, I believe Tuesday's vote could come down to the wire. A boost of momentum for me could prove to my colleagues in Congress that voting against Audit the Fed means angering the nearly 80% of American who support my bill. **As President, auditing the Fed will be a top priority.** [So please stand with me. Please agree to your most generous contribution of $50, $25 or even $10 right away.](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32804176:WHXGG9yGN:m:1:879035888:E65B9A12DF14456000F3288859BBAAA3:r) In liberty, Rand Paul | | [Chip in $25 >>](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32804174:WHXGG9yGN:m:1:879035888:E65B9A12DF14456000F3288859BBAAA3:r)

name: Rand Paul
subject: I need your help in the early states
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for President
body:
Team Rand,,

I just completed a multi-day swing through
Iowa and New Hampshire, and I couldn't be more excited.

You see, we are in the final stretch before
the first nominating contests, and I can say with confidence that
we've built the best grassroots organization of the field.

We released our 1000th precinct captain in
Iowa and our 500th leadership team endorser in New Hampshire. Our
volunteers are working day in and day out, making 10s of
thousands of calls to spread our positive message far and wide.
Our crowds are large wherever I go, and you can feel the
momentum.

There's no denying - no matter how hard the
media tries to hide it - we have a top tier campaign and we're in
it to win it. The Washington Machine has something to fear. And
it's never been clearer.

But now is when I need your help the most.

If you would be willing to come to Iowa or
New Hampshire for the final 10 days or for any amount of time
before the first contests, please sign up HERE.

If you can't travel to help the campaign with
your time, you still can make a difference by helping sponsor
someone to make the trip for you. Contribute $25 today to help
fund our efforts in vital primary states.

Let's win this thing.

In liberty,

Rand Paul

name: Eric Greitens
subject: My mission to clean up Jefferson City
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
body:
Dear Peter,

Without trust, nothing works. No community, no company, no
church, no charity, no team, can long prosper without trust.

Today, people in Missouri do not trust our leaders. And for good
reason. First, they are completely out of touch. They're focused
on raising taxes, growing government, and caving to special
interests.

Second, too many in Jeff City have forgotten that elected office
is public service. You are supposed to be there as a servant of
the people. Not yourself. Think about it:

Our Attorney General was highlighted as a poster child for
corruption
Our legislators hold official hearings in country clubs while
eating lobbyist-funded fancy food
The House Speaker had a relationship with an intern and was
forced to resign
The crisis at the University of Missouri
The mismanagement of Ferguson
The tragic deaths of Tom Schweich and Spence Jackson
Candidates for the highest office in our state launching secret
smear campaigns
Given all this, is it any surprise that people in Missouri don't
trust our political leaders?

Today, Missouri's policies are shaped by corrupt insiders,
well-paid lobbyists, and self-serving politicians who maximize
their own personal profit, yet continue to fail to serve the
people.

It's time for strong, robust, common sense ethics measures that
will help to restore trust, and return government to "We the
People."

Below are a few of the commonsense policies that I will seek to
put in place as Governor.

#1: Term Limits for Every Statewide Office Holder

Every statewide office in Missouri should be subject to term
limits. This includes the offices of Lieutenant Governor,
Secretary of State, Attorney General, and State Auditor. Missouri
needs proven leaders, not career politicians.

#2: Ban all Gifts from Lobbyists

The culture of corruption in Jefferson City must end. Since 2004,
politicians in Missouri have taken approximately $10.9 million in
gifts from lobbyists. This game of trading lavish gifts and
high-dollar steak dinners for special favors must end. Today in
Missouri, legislators dine out, and when it comes time to pay the
bill, they call in the credit card of a lobbyist. Public office
is about service. Not free meals. Not free tickets. Not free
trips. When I am governor we will ban all gifts from lobbyists.

#3: Close the Revolving Door Between Legislators and Special
Interests

To end the culture of corruption, the revolving door between
legislators and special interests needs to be nailed shut. Too
many politicians try to "cash in" by becoming lobbyists as soon
as they leave office. When I'm Governor, we'll end the culture of
cashing in with a simple rule: for every year you serve in
office, you have to wait a year before you can do paid lobbying
in Missouri. If you serve one year, you have to wait one year
before you can lobby. Serve two, then you wait two. And if you're
a career politician who has been in office for twenty years, then
you wait twenty years before you can engage in paid lobbying.

#4: End Campaign Contributions while the Legislature is in
Session

Today, Missouri's elected officials accept campaign contributions
while the legislature is in session. This means that dollars flow
from special interests to politicians, at the very time that
legislators are considering passing laws to benefit these
contributors. When I'm Governor, we'll establish a level and
clean playing field, so that no state elected official or
candidate for elected office will be able to solicit or accept
campaign contributions while the legislature is in session.

#5: Stop Politicians from Pocketing Campaign Money

Today in Missouri, a politician can raise money to run for
office, drop out of the race, and keep the money. They can then
turn around and use that money to fund a career as a lobbyist or
a "consultant." When I'm Governor, we'll make it law that if a
candidate for office decides not to run, they have to return all
money to their donors within 30 days.

#6: Report All Government Handouts

When I'm Governor, all candidates and elected officials will
report every penny of government tax credits, contracts,
salaries, and special interest handouts that they, their
companies, and their families have received from the Federal and
State government. Missouri is the Show-Me state. It's time for
politicians to be transparent with the people. If a candidate is
in the business of taking special interest tax credits and
contracts, then the people should know about it.

#7: End Donations from Companies Under Investigation

Our Attorney General takes money from the very companies that he
is investigating. It's a sad game. The companies contribute and
the Attorney General does them favors. The companies and the
politicians win; the people lose. When I'm Governor, no company
that is subject to a state lawsuit or investigation will be able
to make campaign contributions to any officeholder or candidate
for office.

#8: Stronger Ethics Enforcement

The Missouri Ethics Commission should have more robust power to
conduct independent investigations. There will be a prosecutor in
the Ethics Commission with the authority to impose stiff
penalties for knowing, willing ethics violations, and harsher
consequences when public trust is breached.

Doing what's right means doing more than just following the law.
Missouri desperately needs to pass ethics legislation - but even
more than that, we need leaders who will set the example. I have
pledged that, as Governor, I will never-for the rest of my
life-engage in paid lobbying. And when I'm Governor, every member
of my staff and every member of my Cabinet will sign a pledge
that they will never engage in lobbying while I am in office.
>From day one, I, my staff, and every member of my team will sign
a pledge that we will never accept gifts from lobbyists.

Missouri deserves leaders who aren't in office for
self-enrichment, and who aren't engaged in sleazy political
games. We deserve leaders who are serious about service.

Trust is established through character and competence. When I'm
Governor, we're going to clean up the mess in Jefferson City,
restore trust in government, and get real results for people.

This is going to be a very big fight. Both parties-Democrats and
Republicans-are at fault, and they are NOT going to like these
reforms, because the games in Jefferson City work just fine for
the sleazy insiders who've gotten rich. They see us coming, and
they know that we're coming after them.

It's a fight I'm happy to take on, because the people deserve a
leader who is serious about doing what's right.

The first step to a more vibrant and hopeful Missouri, full of
great jobs, and providing every child with a world-class
education is creating an open, honest, and ethical environment in
Jeff City. Career politicians have taken us in the opposite
direction. When I'm governor, with this ethics package, we'll
change the course!

Best,

Eric

Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer

When I'm Governor, we'll establish a level and clean playing field, so that no state elected official or candidate for elected office will be able to solicit or accept campaign contributions while the legislature is in session. #5: Stop Politicians from Pocketing Campaign Money Today in Missouri, a politician can raise money to run for office, drop out of the race, and keep the money. They can then turn around and use that money to fund a career as a lobbyist or a "consultant." When I'm Governor, we'll make it law that if a candidate for office decides not to run, they have to return all money to their donors within 30 days. #6: Report All Government Handouts When I'm Governor, all candidates and elected officials will report every penny of government tax credits, contracts, salaries, and special interest handouts that they, their companies, and their families have received from the Federal and State government. Missouri is the Show-Me state. It's time for politicians to be transparent with the people. If a candidate is in the business of taking special interest tax credits and contracts, then the people should know about it. #7: End Donations from Companies Under Investigation Our Attorney General takes money from the very companies that he is investigating. It's a sad game. The companies contribute and the Attorney General does them favors. The companies and the politicians win; the people lose. When I'm Governor, no company that is subject to a state lawsuit or investigation will be able to make campaign contributions to any officeholder or candidate for office. #8: Stronger Ethics Enforcement The Missouri Ethics Commission should have more robust power to conduct independent investigations. There will be a prosecutor in the Ethics Commission with the authority to impose stiff penalties for knowing, willing ethics violations, and harsher consequences when public trust is breached. Doing what's right means doing more than just following the law. Missouri desperately needs to pass ethics legislation - but even more than that, we need leaders who will set the example. I have pledged that, as Governor, I will never-for the rest of my life-engage in paid lobbying. And when I'm Governor, every member of my staff and every member of my Cabinet will sign a pledge that they will never engage in lobbying while I am in office. From day one, I, my staff, and every member of my team will sign a pledge that we will never accept gifts from lobbyists. Missouri deserves leaders who aren't in office for self-enrichment, and who aren't engaged in sleazy political games. We deserve leaders who are serious about service. Trust is established through character and competence. When I'm Governor, we're going to clean up the mess in Jefferson City, restore trust in government, and get real results for people. This is going to be a very big fight. Both parties-Democrats and Republicans-are at fault, and they are NOT going to like these reforms, because the games in Jefferson City work just fine for the sleazy insiders who've gotten rich. They see us coming, and they know that we're coming after them. It's a fight I'm happy to take on, because the people deserve a leader who is serious about doing what's right. The first step to a more vibrant and hopeful Missouri, full of great jobs, and providing every child with a world-class education is creating an open, honest, and ethical environment in Jeff City. Career politicians have taken us in the opposite direction. When I'm governor, with this ethics package, we'll change the course! Best, Eric Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer This message was intended for: dpwillis67+greitens@gmail.com You were added to the system September 30, 2015. [www.DonaldJTrump.com](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.donaldjtrump.com%2F&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=weekinreview2&n=2)
| |
---
\- January 11th, 2016 -
**Donald J. Trump - Week In Review**
| Dear Peter, It’s been an incredible start to the New Year! On Saturday, January 2nd I traveled to Biloxi, Mississippi and hosted a rally attended by almost 15,000 people. I spoke about the issues most important to voters like you and was overwhelmed with support. **** _Speaking to over 13,000 supporters in Biloxi, Mississippi
_ __ On Monday, we released our first campaign television ad that focused on national security, illegal immigration, and Making America Great Again. If you haven’t seen it you can view it [here.](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.donaldjtrump.com%2Fmedia%2Fgreat-again-tv-spot&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=weekinreview2&n=3) On Monday night I was in Lowell, Massachusetts where we had an incredible crowd at the Tsongas Center. 10,000 people waited hours in the freezing cold to listen to me share my vision to rebuild the American Dream and make it bigger and stronger than ever before. It was an amazing night and I am so appreciative of all of you who attended. _Greeting the crowd in Lowell, Massachusetts
_ On Tuesday, we visited Claremont, New Hampshire where I spoke about the issues we face and my common sense solutions. I took questions from voters and loved being in New Hampshire where I have met so many incredible people. _Surrounded an amazing crowd in Claremont, NH

_ On Thursday, I traveled to Burlington, Vermont. It was an interesting visit and I was surprised and honored by the overwhelming support we received. It was a great speech and I am glad we made the trip. _Such tremendous energy from thousands of supporters in Burlington, VT_ On Friday, I returned to South Carolina for a rally in [Rock Hill](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.heraldonline.com%2Fnews%2Flocal%2Fnews-columns-blogs%2Fandrew-dys%2Farticle53942760.html&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=weekinreview2&n=4). We had thousands of people come out to the event and we continue to do really well in all of the South Carolina polls which show me with nearly 38%. _Rock Hill, South Carolina_ I spent all of Saturday in Iowa where I made two speeches. The first stop was in Ottumwa and the second in Clear Lake. We are approaching the Iowa Caucus and I hope everyone turns out to vote. Iowa is where we will begin to turn this country around. I have been winning all my life, and Iowa is so important to me. _Speaking in Ottumwa,_ Iowa
_Meeting voters in Clear Lake, Iowa_ On Sunday I traveled west for a rally in Reno, Nevada where we had 5,000 people in attendance. It was a really enjoyable event and I look forward to visiting Nevada more often. _Greeting the supporters in Reno, Nevada_ Also this week, I am on the cover of [TIME Magazine](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.donaldjtrump.com%2Fmedia%2Fdonald-trumps-art-of-the-steal&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=weekinreview2&n=5), which is a great honor. The story is one of the best I’ve ever had. For once the media is honest about what is going on--- it’s a movement! This week I will travel to [Windham, New Hampshire](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.eventbrite.com%2Fe%2Fdonald-j-trump-in-windham-nh-tickets-20482376339&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=weekinreview2&n=6); [Cedar Falls, Iowa](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.eventbrite.com%2Fe%2Fdonald-j-trump-in-cedar-falls-ia-tickets-20442150021&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=weekinreview2&n=7); [Pensacola, Florida](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.eventbrite.com%2Fe%2Fdonald-j-trump-in-pensacola-fl-tickets-20477021322&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=weekinreview2&n=8); Charleston, South Carolina for the Fox Business debate; Dallas County, Iowa and more. I look forward to see each of you on the campaign trail. I thank you for your support and I will never forget that you were with me. Together, we will Make America Great Again!

**Donald J. Trump**

On Saturday, January 2nd I traveled to Biloxi, Mississippi and hosted a rally attended by almost 15,000 people. I spoke about the issues most important to voters like you and was overwhelmed with support. ** Speaking to over 13,000 supporters in Biloxi, Mississippi On Monday, we released our first campaign television ad that focused on national security, illegal immigration, and Making America Great Again. Greeting the crowd in Lowell, Massachusetts On Tuesday, we visited Claremont, New Hampshire where I spoke about the issues we face and my common sense solutions. I took questions from voters and loved being in New Hampshire where I have met so many incredible people. Surrounded an amazing crowd in Claremont, NH On Thursday, I traveled to Burlington, Vermont. We are approaching the Iowa Caucus and I hope everyone turns out to vote. Iowa is where we will begin to turn this country around. I have been winning all my life, and Iowa is so important to me. name: Doug Stafford
subject: The Media Isn't Going to Win
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for President
body:
Dear Patriot,

By any reasonable criteria Rand Paul has a
top tier campaign.

And this coming week, Rand will prove that
with multi-day trips to Iowa and New Hampshire to take his
message of liberty straight to the voters.

Already, Rand's campaign has hit growth marks
other candidates' campaigns never will.

Yet the media continues to work to black
Senator Rand Paul out. This is an outrage. You and I can't let
them get away with this.

Rand will not be relegated to an under card
debate where he clearly doesn't belong. He will not let the media
decide the "tiers" of this race.

Multiple national polls including CNN, CBS,
Fox, Marist and others have Rand in 5th or 6th place. Another had
him just a single point out of 4th.

In multiple polls, Rand scores ahead of Jeb
Bush, Chris Christie, John Kasich and Carly Fiorona.

In yesterday's Marist Iowa poll, he was alone
in 5th place.

His numbers are on the rise in recent weeks
with the race hitting its final stretch, and is set to go even
higher as our campaign flexes our grassroots muscle.

Rand Paul is in it to win it.

He is on the ballot in every state.

He has over 1,000 precinct captains in Iowa
and a huge 500 person leadership team in New Hampshire.

He is an important voice for liberty and has
an important message to share -- a message that's gaining
traction in the early states.

The media won't decide this race. Voters
will.

He will hit the ground running this week to
double down on his efforts to talk straight to the voters.

See you in New Hampshire and Iowa.

In liberty,
Doug Stafford
Chief Political Strategist

name: Trump Virginia - Dena Espenscheid
subject: Donald Trump Volunteer News
body:
Peter --

The campaign to elect Donald Trump for President is in full swing in Virginia. The Trump Train is accelerating down the tracks! Below are ways you can Make America Great Again. Follow the links provided to find your place on the Team Trump in Virginia!
**1\. Phone Banks**
Nightly phone banks are scheduled in cities across Virginia, including:
* Arlington
* Fairfax
* Newport News
* Norfolk
* Richmond
* Virginia Beach

More dates and locations are added daily. Visit [https://www.facebook.com/vafortrump/events](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com%2Fvafortrump%2Fevents&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=vol_news_1_11&n=1) for details on all the events. While you are there like and share the page.
**2\. Phone Bank from Home**
If you want to phone bank, but don’t see one near you, please do the following:
A. Visit [www.DonaldJTrump.com/volunteer](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.DonaldJTrump.com%2Fvolunteer&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=vol_news_1_11&n=2). Look for the phone bank and phone bank from home options, and choose both.
B. E-mail [MidAtlantic@DonaldTrump.com](mailto:MidAtlantic@DonaldTrump.com) to request instructions and lists.
**3.[www.DonaldJTrump.Com/Volunteer](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.DonaldJTrump.Com%2FVolunteer&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=vol_news_1_11&n=3)**
All Volunteers and supporters must register at [www.DonaldJTrump.com/volunteer](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.DonaldJTrump.com%2Fvolunteer&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=vol_news_1_11&n=4) to let us know how you want to help. Please share this link on social media as well.
**4\. Election Day Volunteers**
We will need help on March 1st, as well as the night before to win the Virginia Republican Primary Election. Let us know you are able to join in by registering on our [Election Day Volunteer Eventbrite Page](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.eventbrite.com%2Fe%2Felection-day-volunteers-for-donald-j-trump-tickets-20702987192&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=vol_news_1_11&n=5). [
](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.eventbrite.com%2Fe%2Felection-day-volunteers-for-donald-j-trump-tickets-20702987192&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=vol_news_1_11&n=6)
All Election Day volunteers must also
1. Register at [www.DonaldJTrump.com/Volunteer](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.DonaldJTrump.com%2FVolunteer&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=vol_news_1_11&n=7)
2. Vote absentee. Check your registration and request an absentee ballot at the [Virginia Voter Information Portal](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fvote.elections.virginia.gov%2FVoterInformation&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=vol_news_1_11&n=8)

**5\. Mass Units, District Conventions, State Conventions**
The process to win the primary election is more than just the Primary Election. The Donald Trump for President Team in Virginia also has to win delegates to the National Convention in Cleveland. We need as many supporters as possible to flood the local GOP Unit Mass Meetings, District Conventions and State Convention. For more information on meetings in your area, visit: [Virginia GOP Unit Mass Meeting & Convention Information Page](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.eventbrite.com%2Fe%2Fvirginia-unit-mass-meeting-district-convention-state-convention-tickets-20022778670&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=vol_news_1_11&n=9)
**6\. 4x8 Signs**
If you own or control a location for a 4x8 sign, please e-mail us at [MidAtlantic@DonaldTrump.com](mailto:MidAtlantic@DonaldTrump.com) to give us written permission to install a sign. Thank you!
**7\. Used Computers and Tablets**
The Campaign can make use of your used, unwanted electronics. If they are small enough to send, please send them to the Richmond office at:
Donald J. Trump for President
10015 West Broad St
Glen Allen, VA 23060
The latest polling shows **[Mr. Trump holds a 9 point lead in Virginia with 28%](http://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fovertimepolitics.com%2Ftrump-holds-a-9-point-lead-in-virginia-over-ted-cruz-28-19%2F&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=vol_news_1_11&n=10)**. Together, we can make this number even greater come Election Day!

Thank you for helping to Make America Great Again!
\- Dena
Dena Espenscheid
Virginia Field Director
Donald J. Trump For President, Inc.

Paid for by Donald J. Trump for President, Inc.

---

name: Rand Paul
subject: Are you kidding me?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for President
body:
Dear Patriot,
I know the media and the Washington machine
have been trying to block our message, but I didn't think they'd
go this far.
This week we proved the strength of our top
tier campaign with multi-day trips to Iowa and New Hampshire to
take my message of liberty straight to the voters.
I spoke with packed crowds and it's never
been more clear: we have the support we need in Iowa and New
Hampshire.
But those supporters don't mean enough to the
media. We just learned today that we will not be allowed on the
mainstage at Thursday's debate.
That's because the media doesn't care about
the truth, they care about their own agenda. They want to decide
the "tiers" of this race and name the winners and losers.
I will not stand for this. In multiple
national polls including CNN, CBS, Fox, Marist … We're 5th
or 6th place! We're ahead of Bush, Christie, Kasich, and Fiorina.
And our numbers continue to grow. We now have
over 1,000 precinct captains in Iowa and a huge 500 person
leadership team in New Hampshire.
I will not accept this artificial designation
of the success of our campaign.
I am taking this campaign to the voters -
Iowa, New Hampshire, and beyond.
The media won't decide this race. Voters
will.
I'm in this to win it, but I need your help
now more than ever. Will you stand with me today?
In liberty,
Rand Paul

clean_body: Dear Patriot, I know the media and the Washington machine have been trying to block our message, but I didn't think they'd go this far. This week we proved the strength of our top tier campaign with multi-day trips to Iowa and New Hampshire to take my message of liberty straight to the voters. I spoke with packed crowds and it's never been more clear: we have the support we need in Iowa and New Hampshire. But those supporters don't mean enough to the media. We just learned today that we will not be allowed on the mainstage at Thursday's debate. That's because the media doesn't care about the truth, they care about their own agenda. They want to decide the "tiers" of this race and name the winners and losers. I will not stand for this. In multiple national polls including CNN, CBS, Fox, Marist … We're 5th or 6th place! We're ahead of Bush, Christie, Kasich, and Fiorina. And our numbers continue to grow. We now have over 1,000 precinct captains in Iowa and a huge 500 person leadership team in New Hampshire. I will not accept this artificial designation of the success of our campaign. I am taking this campaign to the voters - Iowa, New Hampshire, and beyond. The media won't decide this race. Voters will. I'm in this to win it, but I need your help now more than ever. Will you stand with me today?
